headlines.title: Trump admin appears to back off $1.8B ‘anti-weaponization’ fund after rare GOP backlash
headlines.description: The Trump administration signaled it is backing off on the creation of a $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund announced by the Justice Department that could send money to allies of President Donald Trump.

headlines.title: Primary challengers threaten to unseat House members across the country
headlines.description: Four House members have already lost re-election bids so far this year, just a few months into primary season.

headlines.title: 30 gallons of fuel spilled on National Mall after event for America’s 250th birthday
headlines.description: WASHINGTON – More than 30 gallons of fuel spilled onto the National Mall from generators that were used during a recent event celebrating America’s 250th anniversary that was organized by Freedom 250, a group created by the Trump administration, according to three people familiar with the matter

headlines.title: Democrats think their secret sauce in 2026 is targeting Trump and GOP on ‘corruption’
headlines.description: Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro decried corruption during his primary night address to supporters in Bucks County last month.

headlines.title: Trump agenda faces challenges as DOJ drops ‘anti-weaponization’ fund and Iran talks appear to stall
headlines.description: President Trump is expected to drop his $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund after bipartisan backlash. It comes as Iran says it is suspending peace talks after Israel expanded its military operations in Lebanon. NBC News correspondents Mel Zanona, Garrett Haake, Richard Engel, Courtney Kube and Allie Canal report on the latest developments.

headlines.title: Trump to drop controversial 'anti-weaponization' fund
headlines.description: President Trump is expected to make an announcement about plans to drop the Justice Department's "anti-weaponization" fund. NBC News' Melanie Zanona has details on the backlash the fund has faced on Capitol Hill.

headlines.title: Trump says talks between the U.S. and Iran continue 'at a rapid pace'
headlines.description: In a post on social media, President Trump said that talks between the United States and Iran have been continuing "at a rapid pace," not long after Iranian state media had said the high-stakes negotiations had been suspended. NBC News' Monica Alba and Richard Engel have details on the evolving situation.

headlines.title: Massive leadership vacuum weakens U.S. diplomacy in the face of global crises
headlines.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: Sen. Chuck Schumer lays out plan to fight Trump’s ‘anti-weaponization’ fund in Congress
headlines.description: The Senate minority leader said Democrats would use Senate procedure to try to kill President Donald Trump’s $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, forcing Republicans to vote to preserve the compensation money.

headlines.title: Graham Platner’s wife ‘angry’ disclosures of his extramarital sexting were made public
headlines.description: The wife of Graham Platner, the presumptive Democratic nominee for the Senate in Maine, said she is “really angry” about reports that she previously told her husband’s campaign he had exchanged sexually explicit texts with other women.“It makes me really angry, disappointed,” Platner’s wife, Amy Gertner, said in a direct-to-camera video Platner’s campaign released Saturday night.
